title: BlockBlast
description: I developed a high-performance, grid-based puzzle engine in Unity, focusing on efficient object pooling, complex combo logic, and a data-driven level system to ensure a smooth 'tap-to-blast' experience.
textOne: I built BlockBlast to master the mechanics of modern casual puzzle games. I implemented a robust grid system that handles color-matching logic and recursive 'blast' algorithms. My goal was to create a satisfying game feel through snappy animations and well-timed chain reactions using DOTween for all movement and scaling effects.
textTwo: I designed a data-driven level system that allows for unique layouts and objectives without hardcoding._I implemented custom Special Item logic for Rockets and TNT, including a complex 'combination' system (e.g., TNT + Rocket cross-clears)._To ensure mobile performance, I used a specialized ParticlePoolManager for zero-allocation visual effects during heavy explosions._I also built a deterministic board refill system with gravity-based physics to maintain game state consistency.
textThree: The core of my implementation relies on an efficient adjacent-search algorithm to detect matching cubes._I integrated a 'Move Limit' and 'Objective Tracker' system that monitors player progress in real-time._I focused heavily on the UI/UX flow, ensuring that spawning specials feels rewarding through dynamic particle triggers and smooth UI Toolkit transitions.

title: Kiss Cargo
description: Kiss Cargo is a chaotic co-op fairy-tale adventure where you and your friends carry Snow White to the Prince before the world around you falls apart. What starts as a simple rescue turns into a hilarious, high-pressure journey through collapsing bridges, deadly traps, enemy ambushes, and teamwork-driven puzzles where every step can turn heroism into disaster.
textOne: At Blay Games, I worked as a Gameplay Programmer on Kiss Cargo. I took full ownership of several player-side and game-critical systems, delivering them with a strong focus on high performance and long-term reliability.
textTwo: I designed and implemented the Player State Manager and transition logic from the ground up._I built the complete trap system and its gameplay interactions._I developed the player's stamina flow, ghost mode, and ragdoll systems._I also created the princess stamina system together with balancing hooks to support gameplay tuning.
textThree: I integrated gameplay-triggered VFX and audio systems to ensure cohesive player feedback._Most importantly, I developed every assigned feature to be fully compatible with Normcore._This ensured stable synchronization, predictable behavior, and smooth multiplayer gameplay across all implemented systems.
companyResponsibilities: Player State Manager and transition behavior
Trap systems and gameplay interactions
Player and princess stamina systems
Post-death ghost mode and player ragdoll implementation
VFX and audio integrations
Multiplayer-ready implementation of all delivered systems with Normcore
Core gameplay system ownership from development to in-game stability
Player-focused system design with reliability and performance priorities

title: GOKA Street
description: GOKA Street is a social, high-energy street football experience where every match feels alive from the first second to the final goal. You build your own style, unlock bold character-driven abilities, and compete in fast multiplayer games where momentum can change instantly, making each match feel personal, unpredictable, and intensely competitive.
textOne: I delivered major game systems from the concept stage to final implementation. While I contributed to design discussions, I independently executed the development of core UI systems, backend transition workflows, and key gameplay features to ship a polished player experience.
textTwo: I built the card-based boost system and the Goal Frenzy mechanic from scratch._I implemented the Dynamic Progress Road system end-to-end, from player-facing flow to underlying systems._This included target tracking logic, reward pricing behavior, and progression state handling.
textThree: I led the technical migration from Firebase to Nakama and built UI flows using UI Toolkit._I also implemented robust offline mode behavior, online re-entry handling, and re-sync pipelines._All systems were developed to stay stable and correctly synchronized in multiplayer with Normcore.
companyResponsibilities: Card-based boost and Goal Frenzy systems
Dynamic road progression and pricing logic
UI Toolkit implementation tasks
Firebase to Nakama migration support
Offline/online transition and reload flows
Multiplayer-ready implementation of delivered features with Normcore
End-to-end implementation of major progression, UI, and gameplay systems
Backend transition framework implementation for stable live game flow

title: AR Mobile Application (TÜBİTAK Project)
description: I engineered this Unity-based AR mobile application from scratch as part of a TÜBİTAK collaboration, focusing on real-world environment tracking and interactive AR gameplay mechanics.
textOne: Working under the guidance of Assoc. Prof. Ayşegül, I took full responsibility for the technical execution of this AR project. I transformed the conceptual design into a functional, production-ready mobile application, managing the entire development lifecycle from AR foundation setup to App Store deployment.
textTwo: I built the core AR flow, ensuring stable image/plane tracking and object persistence within the mobile environment._I developed the application's architecture to handle real-time user interactions and gamified archaeological educational content._I independently managed the feature implementation, integration of 3D assets, and rigorous testing cycles.
textThree: I successfully delivered a production-ready application for iOS, meeting all TÜBİTAK project milestones._Throughout the process, I maintained a close feedback loop with the academic lead to validate educational accuracy and technical performance._I handled the final production handoff and technical documentation for the project's long-term sustainability.
companyResponsibilities: End-to-end implementation based on provided planning/design
AR application implementation and technical integration
Testing, iteration, and release preparation
Collaboration with Assoc. Prof. Ayşegül

title: Zombie Farmer
description: I developed this fast-paced Top-Down Shooter to experiment with wave-based survival mechanics, enemy AI behavior, and tight combat loops within a time-constrained gameplay environment.
textOne: In Zombie Farmer, I focused on creating an intense 30-second survival loop. I built the project to understand the balance between player movement speed and enemy horde density, ensuring that the 2D/3D combat felt responsive and the 'ticking clock' pressure was palpable for the user.
textTwo: I implemented a dynamic spawning system where zombie waves increase in difficulty as the player progresses._I built an extensible weapon system that allows for various attack types, from melee pitchforks to projectile-based shotguns._I integrated a global timer and score manager to drive player engagement and replayability through high-score chasing.
textThree: I developed the player controller using Unity's input system for smooth navigation across the farm terrain._I focused on visual feedback by integrating particle effects for combat and clear UI indicators for the countdown timer._The project served as a foundation for my understanding of game state management and win/loss condition logic.

title: Unlocker's Tale
description: I created this 3D third-person adventure to explore diverse gameplay styles from exploration to dodging and time-trial puzzles all within a single narrative progression.
textOne: Unlocker's Tale was my journey into multi-genre game development. I designed three distinct levels that each require a different mechanical approach: House (Exploration), City (Reflexes), and Maze (Strategy). My focus was on creating a cohesive experience where the player’s goal finding the key remains constant while the environment changes entirely.
textTwo: In Level 2, I implemented a 'lane-based' obstacle avoidance system with moving vehicles and persistent coin collection logic._In Level 3, I developed a maze navigation system with a countdown timer, utilizing Unity’s navigation mesh for potential AI but focusing on player pathfinding and orientation challenges._I built the key/door interaction system to be modular, working across different level contexts and UI prompts.
textThree: I integrated a global Game Over and Restart system that preserves certain stats (like coins) while resetting level progress._I utilized 3D character controllers and camera transitions to maintain a consistent third-person feel throughout the shifting environments._This project sharpened my skills in level-specific state management and UI-driven storytelling.

title: Desert Run
description: I built this endless runner to master procedural environment generation and dynamic difficulty scaling in a mobile-first 3D environment.
textOne: Desert Run was a technical exercise in 'infinite' systems. I developed a pathway generation algorithm that spawns desert terrain and obstacles ahead of the player while cleaning up past objects to maintain high performance. I focused on making the speed ramp feel natural, increasing the challenge as the player survives longer.
textTwo: I implemented mobile-ready touch controls (swipes for steering and jumping) using an intuitive gesture detection system._I built a power-up framework, including speed boosts and invincibility states, that temporary modifies the player's collision layers and movement parameters._I integrated a high-score leaderboard system to encourage competitive play.
textThree: I designed a modular obstacle system to spawn cacti, rocks, and fallen trees at randomized but fair intervals._I utilized Unity's animation system and DOTween to create smooth character transitions between lanes._This project deepened my understanding of object lifecycle management and mobile performance optimization.

title: Starship Sprint
description: I developed this 3D arcade platformer to focus on physics-based movement and precision landing mechanics in a space-themed setting.
textOne: In Starship Sprint, my primary goal was to fine-tune 'weighty' physics. I wanted the starship to feel like it had momentum, requiring the player to counter-thrust and carefully manage their trajectory. I designed several levels that force players to navigate through narrow debris fields before reaching a precise landing target.
textTwo: I implemented a physics-driven starship controller with thrust and rotation logic._I built a 'Landing Zone' detection system that calculates the angle and velocity of the ship to determine a successful touchdown vs. a crash._I developed a level manager that handles transitions and stores player scores based on time and landing precision.
textThree: I used Unity’s particle systems to create engine exhaust and space environments to enhance the arcade feel._I designed the UI to provide real-time feedback on fuel (if applicable) and ship status._This project allowed me to experiment with 3D gravity anomalies and obstacle-based level design.

title: Dino
description: I recreated the classic Chrome Dino experience in Unity to practice 2D sprite management, parallax scrolling, and mobile-optimized input loops.
textOne: Dino was a project focused on 'gameplay feel' and retro-mechanics. I rebuilt the iconic browser game for mobile, ensuring the jump height and gravity felt identical to the original. I focused on the core loop: run, jump, duck (where applicable), and avoid, while maintaining a clean, minimalist aesthetic.
textTwo: I implemented a parallax background system that moves at different speeds to create a sense of depth in a 2D space._I built a randomized obstacle spawner for cacti and birds that adjusts frequency based on the player's current speed._I integrated a high-score system that persists across sessions, mimicking the 'one-more-try' addictive nature of the original.
textThree: I optimized the game for mobile with simple tap-to-jump controls that respond with zero lag._I managed the game's difficulty curve by gradually increasing the global time scale as the player's score rises._This project was a great lesson in 2D physics and mobile UI simplicity.

title: Susuwatari
description: I developed this mobile game inspired by Flappy Bird, focusing on smooth physics-based flight, scoring systems, and polished UI animations.
textOne: With Susuwatari, I wanted to practice the 'Flappy' mechanic while adding my own visual style. I implemented a physics-based jump system where the player must fight against constant gravity. My focus was on the collision precision ensuring that passing through pipes felt fair and hitting them felt impactful.
textTwo: I built a procedural pipe spawning system that varies the gap heights to keep the gameplay unpredictable._I integrated a score tracking system that triggers UI animations and sound effects upon passing through pipes._I developed a high-score persistence system using PlayerPrefs to track the user's best performance.
textThree: I used Unity’s 2D physics engine for Susuwatari’s movement, fine-tuning the upward force for a snappy feel._I implemented the UI transitions and restart flows to be quick, minimizing the time between death and the next attempt._This project helped me master the basics of 2D game loops and mobile deployment.

title: Syllabus Conversion and Management Application
description: I developed this Java-based desktop application to solve a real-world administrative problem: converting complex syllabus data into structured JSON for system integration.
textOne: As a Computer Engineering project, I built this application to streamline how educators manage academic records. I focused on data integrity and ease of use, creating a tool that could take various input formats and output a unified, OASIS-compatible JSON structure while maintaining a transparent history of changes.
textTwo: I implemented a version control system that requires users to provide justifications for any syllabus modifications._I developed a robust JSON parser and exporter to ensure the output met strict architectural requirements for external database integration._I built a side-by-side comparison tool that allows users to examine differences between two stored JSON versions.
textThree: I designed the desktop interface using JavaFX, focusing on a clean and intuitive user experience for non-technical users._I integrated file I/O operations to handle the import and export of large datasets efficiently._This project strengthened my skills in software architecture, versioning logic, and desktop UI design.
